pre_execution_gate.instruction: Before writing ANY code, read all attached files in full, then produce a structured summary in the chat. The summary MUST include: 1. What feature is being tested and what it does 2. The two profiles under test and their system prompt strategies 3. The test matrix (cache settings × bands × profiles × sequential calls) 4. The primary metric and how TTFT cold/warm-1/warm-2 are measured 5. The PA budget gate and what value triggers PREFIX_CACHE_MANDATORY_PA 6. Speculative decoding compatibility risk (and what to do if incompatible) 7. All governance updates required on completion 8. Pre-condition verification checklist Wait for Lead Architect approval before proceeding.

pre_conditions.check: Branch: `feature/p5-task4-6-prefix-cache` created from merged main HEAD (after merge of `feature/p5-task4-4-xattention-sweep`). Verify HEAD contains Task 4.4 evidence artifact and governance updates. Expected: `phase2_gates/evidence/p5_task4_4_xattention_sweep.json` present.

Task 4.4 evidence parsed for reference TTFT values: - 4K TTFT with XAttention ON: 7,216ms (cold baseline) - 12K TTFT with XAttention ON: 42,390ms (cold baseline) These are the cross-session calibration reference points for Task 4.6.

Verify `enable_prefix_caching` is a valid SchedulerConfig attribute in OpenVINO GenAI 2026.0. Test: `import openvino_genai as ov_genai; s = ov_genai.SchedulerConfig(); hasattr(s, 'enable_prefix_caching')` — must return True. If False: ABORT with `PROPERTY_NOT_AVAILABLE`. Do not attempt workarounds.
Python .venv active with openvino_genai, psutil, transformers.
Test suite baseline: run `python -m pytest --tb=short -q --no-header` and record pass count. Task 4.6 adds NO unit tests (benchmark harness only).

scope.summary: Test whether `SchedulerConfig.enable_prefix_caching` reduces TTFT for sequential calls that share a common system prompt prefix. Measure TTFT cold (no cache populated) vs TTFT warm-1 and warm-2 (cache populated from prior call) for both PA and AO workload profiles at 4K and 12K context bands.
scope.rationale:
Prefix caching stores computed KV activations for prompt tokens in a persistent KV cache. On a second call with the same leading token sequence (the common prefix), the model skips re-computing attention for those tokens and reads their KV values from cache. The benefit is proportional to the length of the shared prefix relative to total input length.
For BlarAI, the system prompt is fixed across all calls within a pipeline session: - PA: a classification instruction prompt (est. 100-300 tokens) - AO: a conversational assistant prompt (est. 30-100 tokens)
If prefix caching works: warm TTFT should drop substantially, particularly for PA where the system prompt represents a significant fraction of typical inputs.
ADR-012 §2.5 stipulates that if PA warm TTFT ≤ 300ms, prefix cache is MANDATORY for the PA production config. Even if 300ms is not achieved, any meaningful TTFT reduction (>10%) that fits within the 2,000ms P95 budget justifies enabling.
IMPORTANT RISK: Speculative decoding may be incompatible with prefix caching in OpenVINO GenAI 2026.0. If the scheduler cannot share KV cache entries between the target model's prefix and draft model states, the feature may silently produce no benefit or cause increased latency. This is the primary risk to manage.
scope.critical_constants: These parameters are LOCKED from prior tasks. Do NOT vary them: - Draft model: Qwen3-0.6B 28L INT4 GPU (Draft-A) — LOCKED (Task 4.2) - num_assistant_tokens: 3 — LOCKED (Task 4.3 DEC-01) - GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION: True — LOCKED (Task 4.4 DEC-05) - KV_CACHE_PRECISION: FP16 (default) — LOCKED (P5-005a) - SchedulerConfig.use_sparse_attention: OFF — DEFERRED (Task 4.3b) - do_sample: False / temperature: 0.0 — project mandate
scope.no_production_code_changes: Task 4.6 touches phase2_gates/ and docs/ ONLY. No modifications to services/, shared/, launcher/, or tests/.

dimension.name: prefix_cache
dimension.note: OFF: scheduler.enable_prefix_caching = False (explicit, not default). ON: scheduler.enable_prefix_caching = True. Each value requires a SEPARATE pipeline compilation (SchedulerConfig is baked in at LLMPipeline construction time — cannot change per-call).

dimension.name: profile
dimension.note:
PA (Policy Agent): compact classification system prompt (~100-200 tokens). PA calls are short bursts: small CAR payload, short output (~3-10 tokens). The max_new_tokens for PA in harness = 32 (current production value — Task 4.8 will optimize; use 32 here to match production-realistic latency profile).
AO (Assistant Orchestrator): conversational system prompt (~30-60 tokens). AO calls are longer: user message + extended generation (~128 tokens). The max_new_tokens for AO = 128 (standard production value).
These profiles test prefix caching at opposite ends of the user-content-to- system-prompt ratio. PA: system prompt is a larger fraction. AO: smaller fraction.

dimension.name: prompt_band
dimension.note: 4096: AO baseline reference band. Spec-decode effective (AR ~0.457). 12288: Near collapse boundary. Spec-decode weakening (AR ~0.378 but TPS low). NOTE: 4K band is large for PA (PA's real production inputs are 512-2K). At 4K band, the system prompt is a SMALL fraction of total input (~5-15%). Prefix cache benefit will be proportionately modest at this band. However, 4K and 12K are specified in P5_TASK4 Task 4.6 scope — use as specified. If the harness detects negligible benefit at 4K, that finding is meaningful data.

dimension.name: sequential_calls
dimension.note: Call 1 (cold): KV cache is empty. Prefix not yet stored. Call 2 (warm-1): System prompt tokens should be in KV cache from Call 1. Call 3 (warm-2): System prompt still in KV cache. All 3 calls use the SAME system prompt text but DIFFERENT user content. Critical: user content must differ between calls to avoid trivial full-prompt cache hits. Only the system prompt prefix should be reused.

test_matrix.total_configs: 4 (2 cache settings × 2 profiles × 2 bands = 8 measurement groups, each group = 3 sequential calls)
test_matrix.pipeline_compilations: 2 (one per prefix_cache setting, shared across profiles/bands)
test_matrix.execution_order:
1. Compile Pipeline A: prefix_cache=OFF (enable_prefix_caching=False) 2. Run PA profile, band=4096: calls [cold, warm-1, warm-2] 3. Run PA profile, band=12288: calls [cold, warm-1, warm-2] 4. Run AO profile, band=4096: calls [cold, warm-1, warm-2] 5. Run AO profile, band=12288: calls [cold, warm-1, warm-2] 6. Calibration check: Pipeline A PA 4K cold TTFT vs Task 4.4 reference (7,216ms ON) 7. Save intermediate Pipeline A results 8. Delete Pipeline A, gc.collect(), time.sleep(3) 9. Compile Pipeline B: prefix_cache=ON (enable_prefix_caching=True) 10. Run PA profile, band=4096: calls [cold, warm-1, warm-2] 11. Run PA profile, band=12288: calls [cold, warm-1, warm-2] 12. Run AO profile, band=4096: calls [cold, warm-1, warm-2] 13. Run AO profile, band=12288: calls [cold, warm-1, warm-2] 14. Compute TTFT deltas (cold vs warm-1, cold vs warm-2) per group 15. Run quality gates 16. Write final evidence artifact
Note: Within each (profile, band) group, the 3 sequential calls MUST be consecutive with no other calls between them and NO pipeline deletion/recreation between calls. The prefix cache accumulates across calls within the same pipeline instance. Preserve sequential ordering within each group.

pipeline_configs.config: scheduler = ov_genai.SchedulerConfig() scheduler.cache_size = 3 scheduler.enable_prefix_caching = False # EXPLICIT false — do not rely on default # sparse_attention OFF (fixed constant — DEFERRED Task 4.3b) pipeline = ov_genai.LLMPipeline( str(target_path), "GPU", scheduler_config=scheduler, draft_model=ov_genai.draft_model(str(draft_path), "GPU"), **{"GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION": True}, # LOCKED Task 4.4 )

scheduler = ov_genai.SchedulerConfig() scheduler.cache_size = 3 scheduler.enable_prefix_caching = True # KEY VARIABLE pipeline = ov_genai.LLMPipeline( str(target_path), "GPU", scheduler_config=scheduler, draft_model=ov_genai.draft_model(str(draft_path), "GPU"), **{"GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION": True}, # LOCKED Task 4.4 )

generation_configs.config: cfg_pa = ov_genai.GenerationConfig() cfg_pa.max_new_tokens = 32 # Current PA production value (Task 4.8 will optimize) cfg_pa.do_sample = False cfg_pa.temperature = 0.0 cfg_pa.top_k = 1 cfg_pa.top_p = 1.0 cfg_pa.num_assistant_tokens = 3 # LOCKED NAT=3 cfg_pa.assistant_confidence_threshold = 0.0 cfg_pa.stop_token_ids = [151645, 151668] # im_end + think — PA Fail-Closed (ADR-012 §2.4)

cfg_ao = ov_genai.GenerationConfig() cfg_ao.max_new_tokens = 128 # Standard AO generation length cfg_ao.do_sample = False cfg_ao.temperature = 0.0 cfg_ao.top_k = 1 cfg_ao.top_p = 1.0 cfg_ao.num_assistant_tokens = 3 # LOCKED NAT=3 cfg_ao.assistant_confidence_threshold = 0.0 cfg_ao.stop_token_ids = [151645] # im_end only — AO standard (ADR-012 §2.4)

test_matrix.prompt_construction:
Tokenizer: AutoTokenizer from Qwen3-14B model directory. Chat template: apply_chat_template with add_generation_prompt=True.
SYSTEM PROMPTS (fixed within profile — same for calls 1, 2, 3):
PA_SYSTEM_PROMPT = ( "You are a security policy enforcement agent. Your task is to classify each " "Action Authorization Request (AAR) into one of exactly three categories: " "ALLOW, DENY, or ESCALATE. " "ALLOW: the requested action is within policy and should be permitted. " "DENY: the requested action violates policy and must be blocked. " "ESCALATE: the request is ambiguous or requires human review before proceeding. " "Output ONLY the label (ALLOW, DENY, or ESCALATE) on a single line. " "Do not include any reasoning, explanation, or additional text. /no_think" ) # Approximate token count: ~120-140 tokens
AO_SYSTEM_PROMPT = ( "You are a helpful AI assistant. Provide clear, accurate, and concise responses. " "/no_think" ) # Approximate token count: ~25-30 tokens
USER CONTENT (varies per call — ensures only system prompt is shared prefix):
For each (profile, band) group, generate 3 different user messages: - user_1: pad to target band token count using unique seed string "CALL_1" - user_2: pad to target band token count using unique seed string "CALL_2" - user_3: pad to target band token count using unique seed string "CALL_3"
Padding pattern: Same as Tasks 4.3/4.4 — repeat a varied phrase pattern to reach target token count. The content of user_1/2/3 must differ so that only the system prompt is the common prefix. Do NOT reuse user_1 content in calls 2/3.
Build all prompts BEFORE compiling Pipeline A (reuse across both pipelines). Verify that tokenized len(prompt_n) ≈ target_band ± 128 for all 3 variants.
KEY API REMINDER: Use list input to generate(): output = pipeline.generate([prompt], gen_config, streamer_callback) NEVER pass bare string — DecodedResults (with metrics) requires list input.

per_call.note: For EACH of the 3 sequential calls per (cache, profile, band) group
per_call.field: First-token wall-clock time (ms). Source: extended_perf_metrics.get_ttft().mean This is the PRIMARY metric for Task 4.6. The cold/warm-1/warm-2 progression directly reveals prefix cache effectiveness.
Pipeline throughput (tokens/sec). Source: extended_perf_metrics.get_throughput().mean Secondary metric — expect minimal TPS difference between cold and warm calls.
Speculative decoding acceptance rate. Source: m_batch_sizes analysis. Expected identical across cold/warm calls. A change in AR between cold and warm calls flags SPEC_DECODE_INTERACTION.
Process RSS high-water mark. Source: RssSampler thread (0.01s polling). Prefix cache consumes additional GPU/CPU memory — track it.
Actual tokens generated (may be < max_new_tokens if EOS fires early, especially for PA). Report exact count per call.

mandatory_measurements.note: Total measurements: 2 pipeline settings × 2 profiles × 2 bands × 3 calls = 24 call records.
mandatory_measurements.validation_rule: A call missing ttft_ms is INVALID. If any group has <2 valid calls, mark that group as INSUFFICIENT_DATA. G-02 counts valid groups.

api_critical_patterns.pattern: output = pipeline.generate([prompt], gen_config, streamer_callback) NEVER: pipeline.generate(prompt, ...) — bare str returns bare str with no metrics.

Within a (profile, band) group, the 3 calls MUST run consecutively on the SAME pipeline instance with NO intervening calls for other profiles/bands. If you interleave profiles (PA_4K_call1 → AO_4K_call1 → PA_4K_call2), the PA system prompt may be evicted from prefix cache before PA call 2. CORRECT order: all 3 calls for (PA, 4K) → then all 3 calls for (PA, 12K) → etc.

epm = output.extended_perf_metrics epm.get_ttft().mean # wall-clock TTFT (ms) — PRIMARY epm.get_throughput().mean # combined TPS epm.get_num_accepted_tokens() # int accepted tokens raw = output.perf_metrics.raw_metrics batch_sizes = list(raw.m_batch_sizes) ar = sum(b-1 for b in batch_sizes) / (3 * len(batch_sizes)) if batch_sizes else 0.0

After compiling Pipeline B (cache=ON), verify the property is active. Method 1: check if Pipeline B cold TTFT at 4K differs from Pipeline A cold TTFT at 4K (compile-time change may shift timing). Method 2: Compare Pipeline B warm-1 TTFT vs Pipeline B cold TTFT — if cache is active, warm-1 must be ≤ cold (never exceed; any benefit shows cache is live). If Pipeline B warm-1 TTFT > Pipeline B cold TTFT at all bands: flag CACHE_OVERHEAD and investigate whether the property has a bug or is unsupported with spec-decode.

calibration_check.instruction: After Pipeline A completes, compare PA 4K cold TTFT with the Task 4.4 reference value (7,216ms — XAttention ON, 4K, cold, from p5_task4_4_xattention_sweep.json). Acceptable range: ±30% (broader range than Task 4.4 due to different max_new_tokens=32 vs 128 and different system prompt structure changing prefill depth). If outside ±30%: flag CALIBRATION_WARNING but proceed. Do not abort for calibration variance — the relative cold/warm comparison within Pipeline B is the primary result.

reference_data.note: Task 4.4 used max_new_tokens=128, AO-style user content. Task 4.6 PA profile uses max_new_tokens=32 and PA system prompt. Cross-task TTFT comparison is indicative only. Within-session cold/warm deltas are reliable.

spec_decode_compatibility_check.instruction:
Prefix caching in speculative decoding pipelines is architecturally complex. The draft model maintains a separate KV cache state that OpenVINO GenAI must coordinate with prefix cache lookups. OpenVINO GenAI 2026.0 may or may not support this fully.
Indicators of INCOMPATIBILITY (document if observed): 1. Pipeline B compilation fails with SchedulerConfig.enable_prefix_caching=True 2. Pipeline B warm-1 TTFT >= Pipeline B cold TTFT at ALL bands AND profiles (no benefit at any measurement point) 3. AR drops to 0.000 in Pipeline B but not Pipeline A (spec-decode disabled by cache) 4. OOM or RuntimeError on first warm call 5. OpenVINO logs a warning about prefix cache incompatibility with speculative decoding
If indicators 1, 3, or 4 are observed: disposition = SPEC_DECODE_INCOMPATIBLE If indicator 2 is observed: disposition = PREFIX_CACHE_NO_BENEFIT If neither: proceed to normal G-01..G-07 evaluation.

quality_gate.gate: All 5 mandatory fields present for all 24 call records. threshold: zero missing ttft_ms values.

A group = (profile, band) triplet = 3 sequential calls. A group is valid if ≥ 2 of 3 calls have valid ttft_ms. Must have all 8 groups (2 profiles × 2 bands × {OFF, ON}) valid. If any group is invalid: disposition = INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE.

For each (profile, band) group in Pipeline B (cache=ON): ttft_cold = call_1.ttft_ms ttft_warm1 = call_2.ttft_ms ttft_warm2 = call_3.ttft_ms reduction_warm1 = (ttft_cold - ttft_warm1) / ttft_cold × 100 reduction_warm2 = (ttft_cold - ttft_warm2) / ttft_cold × 100
Classification per group: reduction_warm1 > 20%: STRONG_BENEFIT reduction_warm1 in [5%, 20%]: MODEST_BENEFIT reduction_warm1 in [-5%, 5%]: NO_BENEFIT (within noise floor) reduction_warm1 < -5%: CACHE_OVERHEAD (warm is slower than cold)
Compare Pipeline B (ON) warm reduction vs Pipeline A (OFF) warm reduction. If Pipeline A (OFF) also shows warm reduction > 5%: scheduler has inherent sequential call optimization even without explicit prefix caching — note this.

For PA profile, band=4096, Pipeline B (cache=ON): If ttft_warm1 ≤ 300ms: flag PA_BUDGET_MET — prefix cache is MANDATORY for PA. If ttft_warm1 <= 1500ms: flag PA_BUDGET_IMPROVED — prefix cache reduces PA latency meaningfully (warm within 2,000ms budget with margin). If ttft_warm1 > 1500ms: note PA warm TTFT is high but report exact value.
IMPORTANT CONTEXT: Based on Task 4.4 reference data (4K cold TTFT ≈ 7-10 seconds) the 300ms threshold from ADR-012 §2.5 was likely derived from a different pipeline measurement methodology. Report the actual measured warm TTFT and let the SDO evaluate against the budget. Do NOT fail the task if 300ms is not achieved.

Speculative decoding acceptance rate should NOT change between cold and warm calls within the same pipeline instance (prefix cache affects prefill, not decode). For each Pipeline B group: if ar_warm1 or ar_warm2 deviates from ar_cold by > 0.05, flag SPEC_DECODE_INTERACTION — unexpected behavior.

Prefix cache consumes memory for stored KV activations. Compare Pipeline B peak RSS at 12K band vs Pipeline A at same band. If delta > 1,000 MB: flag CACHE_MEMORY_OVERHEAD and check against 15,507 MB ceiling. Must not exceed 15,507 MB at any point.
Peak RSS at 12K band for BOTH cache settings must be < 15,507 MB. If exceeded: MEMORY_BUDGET_EXCEEDED.
quality_gate.disposition_rules:
G-01 or G-02 FAIL → INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE
SPEC_DECODE_INCOMPATIBLE (from compatibility check): → dispose SPEC_DECODE_INCOMPATIBLE → Lock prefix_cache=OFF for all production configs. → Document in ADR-012 §2.2 Pipeline kwargs row.
PREFIX_CACHE_MANDATORY_PA (from G-04, ttft_warm1 ≤ 300ms): → PA MUST use prefix cache. AO uses if beneficial. → Update ADR-012 §2.2 Pipeline kwargs row.
G-03 ALL STRONG_BENEFIT or MODEST_BENEFIT for Pipeline B: → disposition = PREFIX_CACHE_BENEFICIAL → Recommend enabling for all profiles. Decision locked in Task 4.10.
G-03 ALL NO_BENEFIT for Pipeline B AND CACHE_OVERHEAD not present: → disposition = PREFIX_CACHE_NO_BENEFIT → Prefix cache does not reduce TTFT. May reflect system prompt being too small relative to input length (system_prompt_tokens / total_tokens < 5%). → Lock prefix_cache=OFF (simpler config, no memory overhead).
G-03 MIXED (some groups STRONG_BENEFIT, others NO_BENEFIT): → disposition = PREFIX_CACHE_CONTEXT_DEPENDENT → Document per-profile/per-band results. Let Task 4.10 lock final config.
G-06 CACHE_MEMORY_OVERHEAD: → Flag for Task 4.10 — memory budget impact must be accounted for in workload profile specifications.

update.condition: always — update Pipeline kwargs row in ADR-012 §2.2

governance_updates.update:
Current row: | Pipeline kwargs | None beyond draft_model | **EVALUATING** | Candidates: scheduler_config, cache_config, device-specific properties. |
Replace with result-conditional text:
If disposition == SPEC_DECODE_INCOMPATIBLE: | Pipeline kwargs | None beyond draft_model | **LOCKED** | Task 4.6: enable_prefix_caching INCOMPATIBLE with speculative decoding in OV GenAI 2026.0 (AR collapse or no benefit detected). Lock OFF for all profiles. Evidence: p5_task4_6_prefix_cache_study.json. |
If disposition == PREFIX_CACHE_MANDATORY_PA: | Pipeline kwargs | enable_prefix_caching=True (PA); True (AO) | **LOCKED** | Task 4.6: PA warm TTFT ≤ 300ms — prefix cache is mandatory per ADR-012 §2.5 budget gate. [Per-band TTFT reduction summary]. Evidence: p5_task4_6_prefix_cache_study.json. |
If disposition == PREFIX_CACHE_BENEFICIAL: | Pipeline kwargs | enable_prefix_caching=True (all profiles) | **LOCKED** | Task 4.6: [reduction summary per profile/band]. Warm TTFT improvement across all tested configs. Enable for all profiles. Evidence: p5_task4_6_prefix_cache_study.json. |
If disposition == PREFIX_CACHE_NO_BENEFIT: | Pipeline kwargs | enable_prefix_caching=False | **LOCKED** | Task 4.6: No measurable TTFT reduction. System prompt fraction too small relative to total input (PA [X]%, AO [Y]%) to produce meaningful cache savings. Evidence: p5_task4_6_prefix_cache_study.json. |
If disposition == PREFIX_CACHE_CONTEXT_DEPENDENT: | Pipeline kwargs | enable_prefix_caching=context-dependent | **EVALUATING** | Task 4.6: Mixed results — [per-profile/band summary]. Task 4.10 will finalize. Evidence: p5_task4_6_prefix_cache_study.json. |
If disposition == INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: Keep row EVALUATING with note: "Task 4.6 INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE — [reason]. Retry pending."

Append new LEDGER entry to docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md. Use the NEXT available entry number (count existing ### Entry N headers + 1). Format:
### Entry [N] — Task 4.6: Prefix Caching Study
**Date:** [actual execution date] **Branch:** `feature/p5-task4-6-prefix-cache` **Commit:** `[HEAD after commit]` **Type:** Empirical Evidence Collection (Configuration Optimization) **Scope:** SchedulerConfig.enable_prefix_caching {OFF, ON} × {PA, AO} profiles × {4096, 12288} context bands. Sequential cold/warm-1/warm-2 TTFT measurement. 24 generate() calls total.
Key findings: - [TTFT cold vs warm-1 reduction % for PA 4K] - [TTFT cold vs warm-1 reduction % for PA 12K] - [TTFT cold vs warm-1 reduction % for AO 4K] - [TTFT cold vs warm-1 reduction % for AO 12K] - [Spec-decode compatibility status] - [RSS overhead from prefix cache] - [PA budget gate result — warm TTFT vs 300ms target]
Quality gate: [G-01..G-07 summary] Disposition: [disposition value]
Evidence: `phase2_gates/evidence/p5_task4_6_prefix_cache_study.json`
Milestone disposition: COMPLETE

execution_prompt.intermediate_save_strategy: Save partial results after EACH (profile, band) group completes (i.e., after each set of 3 sequential calls). Use `p5_task4_6_prefix_cache_study.json.partial`. Save Pipeline A results fully before starting Pipeline B. If Pipeline B compilation fails: save Pipeline A as the final artifact with `spec_decode_compatibility: "SPEC_DECODE_INCOMPATIBLE"` in the analysis section. Delete partial file only after final artifact is written successfully.
execution_prompt.commit_template:
feat(benchmark): Task 4.6 — prefix caching study across PA/AO profiles
Test SchedulerConfig.enable_prefix_caching {OFF, ON} at [4096, 12288] context bands. Sequential cold/warm-1/warm-2 TTFT measurement for PA and AO profiles. 48 generate() calls (24 per cache setting), 2 pipeline compilations.
- PA system prompt: ~[N] tokens / AO system prompt: ~[N] tokens - Speculative decoding compatibility: [COMPATIBLE | SPEC_DECODE_INCOMPATIBLE] - PA 4K TTFT reduction: cold=[X]ms → warm-1=[Y]ms ([Z]%) - AO 4K TTFT reduction: cold=[X]ms → warm-1=[Y]ms ([Z]%) - Disposition: [disposition value] - ADR-012 §2.2 Pipeline kwargs row updated - LEDGER Entry [N]
Evidence: phase2_gates/evidence/p5_task4_6_prefix_cache_study.json

baseline.name: Task 4.4 cold TTFT reference (XAttention ON, for calibration)

reference_data.baseline: Band 4096: TTFT cold = 7,216ms (ON), 9,763ms (OFF) — max_new_tokens=128, AO-style Band 12288: TTFT cold = 42,390ms (ON), 44,655ms (OFF) — max_new_tokens=128, AO-style NOTE: Task 4.6 PA profile uses max_new_tokens=32 and PA system prompt. Actual TTFT at 4K may differ substantially from these values.

PA warm TTFT target: ≤ 300ms (from ADR-012 §2.5 derivation) NOTE: This target may reflect P5-005b measurements from a different methodology. Report the actual measured warm TTFT regardless. The SDO will re-evaluate the budget gate based on actual evidence from this task.

PA system prompt ~130 tokens / 4K total input = ~3.2% shared prefix PA system prompt ~130 tokens / 12K total input = ~1.1% shared prefix AO system prompt ~30 tokens / 4K total input = ~0.75% shared prefix AO system prompt ~30 tokens / 12K total input = ~0.25% shared prefix Implication: small shared prefix fraction means modest absolute TTFT savings. The relative reduction is still meaningful signal about the feature's behavior.

execution_prompt.runtime_estimate: Pipeline A compilation: ~25-30 seconds Pipeline B compilation: ~25-30 seconds Per (profile, band) group: 3 sequential calls At 4K, max_new_tokens=32 (PA): ~3-4 calls × 10-15s each = ~45s per PA 4K group At 4K, max_new_tokens=128 (AO): ~3 × 12-15s = ~40s per AO 4K group At 12K, PA: ~3 × 30-45s = ~2.5 min per group At 12K, AO: ~3 × 45-60s = ~3 min per group Total per pipeline: ~12-14 minutes Both pipelines: ~25-28 minutes With compilations + analysis + save: ~30-40 minutes Ceiling estimate: ~45 minutes (including setup, PC checks, intermediate saves)
execution_prompt.attachment_order: 1. .github/copilot-instructions.md (governance — MUST) 2. docs/Task4.6_v1.xml (this prompt — PRIMARY) 3. docs/P5_TASK4_PRODUCTION_CONFIG_FEASIBILITY.md (spec reference — §Task 4.6) 4. docs/adrs/ADR-012-Qwen3-14B-Model-Selection-Speculative-Decoding.md (parameter table)

command.purpose: Confirm evidence artifact exists and read disposition
Confirm ADR-012 Pipeline kwargs row updated

verification_commands.command: python -c "print([l.strip() for l in open('docs/adrs/ADR-012-Qwen3-14B-Model-Selection-Speculative-Decoding.md') if 'Pipeline kwargs' in l])"
